title: Deterministic Model Port, Scripted Fake, Recordings, and Test Identity · Language Clinic: Request Response Identity
summary: Routine tests call a live model, accept semantic drift as flakiness, overwrite golden responses, and cannot prove which request produced a failure. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the request response identity increment in scripted provider fake and versioned interaction fixture set. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Hash canonical request inputs and correlate every response, step, call, result, error, and usage record to one test case.
Store sanitized versioned provider fixtures with schema, model, API, SDK, timestamp, purpose, and explicit replay limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Request Response Identity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to scripted provider fake and versioned interaction fixture set.

content.code:
# Worked focus: agentpy-request-response-identity — Hash canonical request inputs and correlate every response, step, call, result, error, and usage record to one test case.
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-deterministic-model-port-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-deterministic-model-port-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_deterministic_model_port_3(scripted_turns: int = 18, replayed_turns: int = 18, fixture_versions: int = 3, leaked_live_calls: int = 0, unexplained_differences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if scripted_turns < 8 or replayed_turns < 0:
        return False, replayed_turns, "fake-depth"
    if replayed_turns != scripted_turns:
        return False, replayed_turns, "replay-gap"
    if fixture_versions < 2 or unexplained_differences > 0:
        return False, fixture_versions, "fixture-drift"
    if leaked_live_calls > 0:
        return False, leaked_live_calls, "live-call-leak"
    assert replayed_turns == scripted_turns and leaked_live_calls == 0
    return True, replayed_turns, "deterministic-model-port"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python agent decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected model, interaction, schema, tool, authority, state, approval, memory, evaluation, security, telemetry, sandbox, deployment, or recovery case, then state which provider, Gemini, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, identity, storage, browser, accessibility, privacy, load, cost, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.
